attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- impeccable: Use when the user wants to design, redesign, shape, critique, audit, polish, clarify, distill, harden, optimize, adapt, animate, colorize, extract, or otherwise improve a frontend interface. Covers websites, landing pages, dashboards, product UI, app shells, components, forms, settings, onboarding, and empty states. Handles UX review, visual hierarchy, information architecture, cognitive load, accessibility, performance, responsive behavior, theming, anti-patterns, typography, fonts, spacing, layout, alignment, color, motion, micro-interactions, UX copy, error states, edge cases, i18n, and reusable design systems or tokens. Also use for bland designs that need to become bolder or more delightful, loud designs that should become quieter, live browser iteration on UI elements, or ambitious visual effects that should feel technically extraordinary. Not for backend-only or non-UI tasks.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

file.content:
[package]
name = "hchat"
version = "0.4.1"
edition = "2024"
rust-version = "1.85"
description = "Lightweight desktop chat client for local LLMs"
license = "MIT"
repository = "https://github.com/heath0xFF/hChat"

[dependencies]
eframe = "0.34"
egui_commonmark = "0.23"
reqwest = { version = "0.12", features = ["json", "stream"] }
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["rt-multi-thread", "macros", "sync"] }
tokio-util = "0.7"
futures-util = "0.3"
arboard = "3"
rusqlite = { version = "0.31", features = ["bundled"] }
dirs = "6"
toml = "0.8"
font-kit = "0.14.3"
tiktoken-rs = "0.11.0"

[package.metadata.deb]
maintainer = "Heath"
section = "net"
depends = "$auto"
assets = [
    ["target/release/hchat", "usr/bin/", "755"],
]

[package.metadata.bundle]
name = "hChat"
identifier = "com.heath.hchat"
category = "Utility"

[profile.release]
strip = true

[profile.dist]
inherits = "release"
opt-level = "s"
lto = true

1. Reorder so left/right sidebars come before the bottom input panel (so bottom panel only fills the middle)
2. Increase the input box height

Let me make the input box taller first.

content.text:
Structure looks correct. Panel order is now: top → left sidebar → right context sidebar → bottom input panel → central. In egui, each panel constrains the remaining space, so the bottom panel will only span the area between the two sidebars and the sidebars will extend down to the window edge.

Changes made in `src/app.rs`:
- Moved the `// Bottom input panel` block to after both sidebar panels, so the sidebars now reserve full vertical space first and the input panel only spans the central column between them.
- Bumped the input box height from `35.0` → `90.0` and `desired_rows(1)` → `desired_rows(4)`.

I couldn't find `cargo` on PATH to compile-check, so please run `cargo run` to verify visually. If the input feels too tall/short, the height in `app.rs:1130` is the knob to adjust.
i actually am on a new laptop and had to install rust. test now yeah?

content.text:
Clean build, no errors or warnings. The layout changes compile correctly.

To see it visually, run:

```
cargo run --release
```

If anything feels off (input too tall, sidebars too narrow, etc.), the knobs are:
- Input height: `app.rs:1130` (currently `90.0`) and `desired_rows` on line 1133 (currently `4`)
- Left sidebar width: `app.rs:874` (`default_size(200.0)`)
- Right context sidebar width: `app.rs:1056` area (`default_size(250.0)`)
looks good. tag a new minor release and push it.

chore: bump version to 0.5.0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git tag -a v0.5.0 -m "v0.5.0" && git log --oneline -3 && git tag --sort=-v:refname | head -3
